name: Compute Station-Epicenter Distances for the Noto Earthquake
description:
A seismologist at the GYM Seismology Lab needs to determine which stations in the GE network are closest to the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake epicenter. Calculate the great-circle distance (in km) from the epicenter to each GE network station (TOLI, GSI, KWP, SANI, BKB) using the Haversine formula (d = 2R·arcsin(√(sin²(Δlat/2) + cos(lat1)·cos(lat2)·sin²(Δlon/2))) where R = 6371 km).

Export a CSV file to ~/Documents/station_distances.csv with the header station,network,latitude,longitude,distance_km, containing all five GE stations sorted by ascending distance. Create a summary report at ~/Documents/distance_summary.txt containing the earthquake origin parameters (latitude, longitude, depth, origin time), the nearest and farthest station names with their distances, and the total number of stations processed.

The SeisComP database (mysql -u sysop -psysop seiscomp; tables: Origin, Station, Network, Event, PublicObject), CLI tools (scinv, scevtls, scorgls), and Python 3 are available for use. Distances must be accurate to within ±5 km.
